mainEntity.name: How much does an AJ20D6 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Diesel) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ20D6 Ingenium diesel typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, varying by power output and specification. Used units can be found from £2,500–£4,500, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the AJ20D6 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Diesel) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 is a modern and capable straight-six diesel (4.0★) with strong real-world feedback. Early examples (2020–2021) have reported AdBlue system and EGR cooler issues, but JLR has addressed these with software updates and hardware revisions.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common AJ20D6 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: AdBlue system issues (NOx sensors, heaters, and crystallisation) are the most common reported problems, particularly on early 2020–2021 vehicles. EGR cooler leaks and occasional DPF clogging from urban use are also reported. No major bottom-end issues are known.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the AJ20D6?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 powers the latest-generation Range Rover (L460), Range Rover Sport (L461), and Defender (L663) — spanning D250, D300, and D350 outputs.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover AJ20D6 the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the AJ20D6 is shared with Jaguar fitments in the F-Pace, XF, and XJ. The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.
